summary_en: ROE 5.7% for FY3/26 (up from 4.4% prior year). PBR 1.25x, PER 22.9x. Net D/E 0.39x. Company targets sustained ROE improvement through fresh seafood volume expansion, logistics rationalization, DX, and organizational strengthening. Medium-term plan MF-2026 Year 2 review shows selective progress on new customer acquisition and Group-wide product development; logistics cost inflation and near-term plan discontinuations noted.
title_en: Progress Report on Initiatives Toward Management Conscious of Cost of Capital and Stock Price
translation_en_brief: On 14 May 2026, Tsukiji Uoichiba Company filed a progress report on capital-cost and stock-price conscious management in response to Tokyo Stock Exchange guidance. The company reported ROE of 5.7% for the fiscal year ending March 2026 (up from 4.4% in the prior year), with net profit margin at 0.6%, PBR at 1.25x, and net debt-to-equity at 0.39x. The report notes that ROE has been influenced by extraordinary gains on securities sales; however, underlying seafood wholesale operations showed signs of improvement offset by elevated loan loss provisions in consolidated subsidiaries. The company is pursuing transformation from a traditional consignment wholesaler to a full-service distributor. Under its medium-term plan MF-2026 (launched April 2024), the company has achieved selective progress: new customer acquisition and sales channel diversification are expanding; collaboration with Group processing affiliates (Kyodo Suisan and Kita-Shoku) is being strengthened for product development; and logistics inside and outside the market continue to be rationalized. However, labour cost inflation is outpacing logistics efficiency gains, and certain planned initiatives have been abandoned. The company commits to further DX deployment and industry-wide logistics coordination to improve operational efficiency and profitability.
